markdownRemark.html:
Query Condition Data Source

Used internally in a DataSet to store data corresponding to query conditions, making it easier for developers to handle query condition data. When executing dataSet.query, the current record data in the queryDataSet will be converted into query conditions.

Basic Usage

const queryDS = new DataSet({
  fields: [
    { name: 'name', type: 'string' },
    { name: 'age', type: 'number' },
  ],
});
const dataSet = new DataSet({
  fields: [
    { name: 'name', type: 'string' },
    { name: 'age', type: 'number' },
  ],
  queryDataSet: queryDS,
});

Applicable Scenarios

When developers want to manage or retrieve query condition information more flexibly, they can instantiate a queryDataSet themselves and set default values for query fields, whether query conditions are editable, etc.

Related

DataSet Props queryFields

Property Description Type Default
queryFields Array of query field properties; internally generates a queryDataSet, taking precedence over the queryDataSet property. See Field Props for details. object[] 
const dataSet = new DataSet({
  fields: [
    { name: 'name', type: 'string' },
    { name: 'age', type: 'number' },
  ],
  queryFields: [
    { name: 'name', type: 'string' },
    { name: 'age', type: 'number' },
  ],
});

Using queryFields will automatically generate a queryDataSet inside the dataSet, and you can also access the automatically generated query data source through dataSet.queryDataSet.

Give it a try

Click query to see the corresponding information for data and params.

Note: The official website data uses a mock interface, and no interface has been set up to query based on conditions. You can check the query condition information output in the console.
